name: German Chocolate Cake (Dupree)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Butter 3 round layer pans (8 or 9 inches) or 2 square pans (8x8x2 inches or 9x9x2 inches).
3. Line bottoms of pans with wax paper and butter paper.
4. Pour 2 cups boiling water over chocolate, stirring until melted; set aside to cool.
5. In a large mixing bowl beat butter and sugar together until light.
6. Add egg yolks one at a time, beating after each addition.
7. Blend in melted chocolate and vanilla extract.
8. Blend flour, baking soda, and salt separately.
9. Alternately mix the flour mixture and buttermilk into the butter mixture, beating after each addition until batter is smooth.
10. Fold in egg whites and pecans.
11. Divide batter among prepared pans.
12. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes (8-inch round layers), 30 to 35 minutes (9-inch round layers), 45 to 50 minutes (8-inch squares), or 40 to 45 minutes (9-inch squares).
13. Cool in pans on wire racks.
14. Remove from pans and make frosting:
- In a heavy saucepan combine evaporated milk, sugar, egg yolks, and butter.
- Cook and stir over medium heat until thick, about 12 minutes.
- It should have come to a gentle boil. Stir in vanilla extract, coconut flakes, and pecans.
- Beat with a mixer until thick and cold enough to spread.
15. Spread frosting on each layer and top of cake.
16. Wrap the cake in strips of wax paper before serving.
Yield: 1 cake
